source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A DC voltage source is connected to a series L-C circuit by turning on the switch S at time \(t=0\) as shown in the figure. Assume \(i(0)=0\), \(v(0)=0\). Which one of the following circular loci represents the plot of \(i(t)\) versus \(v(t)\)?

choices in the record are only represented as A/B/C/D labels without descriptive option text

issues: metadata.extraction_notes state that no image path/url is provided, which is inconsistent with the top-level image_paths and fallback image_manifest
metadata.image_paths is empty while top-level image_paths contains one image path

image_dependency: Question explicitly says 'as shown in the figure'
The task asks which circular loci matches i(t) versus v(t), so the option content is likely graphical rather than text-described
asset_registry_record.image_manifest shows one existing PNG image
multi_step: The problem combines circuit interpretation (series L-C with DC switching) and transient analysis with initial conditions i(0)=0, v(0)=0
To answer, a solver must derive or recognize the relationship between i(t) and v(t) and then map that result to one of the plotted loci
This is more than direct lookup because it involves physics/equation reasoning plus option matching
verifiability: answer_type is option/choice and answer_text is a single clear label 'B'
Initial conditions and circuit type are explicitly specified, enabling analytical checking of the phase-plane locus
asset_registry_record.registry_passed=true, with question and answer both present
